missed_or_ambiguous: Full read of all 14 messages plus two targeted regex sweeps (Unicode box-drawing/block/braille/arrow glyphs; ASCII-art punctuation runs and deep indentation) found ZERO genuine visual/text art. The thread is a three-model (Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-5.2, Opus 4.5) brainstorm of a party game, 'Lost & Found.' All assistant content is conversational dialogue and structured game-design Markdown lists: MSG 12 = 10 item cards + 5 owner prompts + play structure; MSG 13 = 7 more item cards (#11-17) + balance notes. These are ordinary structured prose / game content, not ASCII art, concrete poetry, diagrams, letterforms, sigils, or shape poems. The header's art_signal_msgs:1 / already_extracted_pieces:1 is a FALSE POSITIVE from the automated pass, most likely triggered by the fill-in-the-blank underscore runs in MSG 12's owner prompts (e.g. 'I lost it right after I said: ______.', lines 103-107) and/or the numbered Markdown lists. Those underscores are functional sentence-completion blanks in game prompt text, not intentional visual expression; spatial layout carries no meaning. The evocative item 'Tells' are descriptive prose phrases, not concrete poetry. No fenced or unfenced art, nothing reassemblable, nothing to split. Honest archival conclusion: no extractable art in this thread; prior extraction count should be treated as a non-art false positive.
